points.definition: AI as 'Layer 8' — the agentic layer that mediates between you and everything else on the internet, negotiating on our behalf, filtering information, and determining how we interact with the digital world.
points.citation: Now AI is becoming the new intermediary. It's what I've started calling "Layer 8" — the agentic layer that mediates between you and everything else on the internet.

points.name: Mustafa Suleyman
points.definition: A general-purpose learning system that can perform well across all human-level training environments, including knowledge work and physical labor, without extensive handcrafted prompting.
points.citation: To me, AGI is a general-purpose learning system that can perform well across all human-level training environments. So, knowledge work, by the way, that includes physical labor.

points.name: Mythos Ventures
points.definition: Radically more advanced and general AI systems that exceed human performance on virtually all cognitive tasks
points.citation: Absent regulatory limits, we expect capabilities of future systems to exceed human performance on virtually all cognitive tasks.

points.name: Naomi Klein
points.definition: AGI is defined as the point at which the majority of human economic activity is replaced by machines
points.citation: We're talking about this very specific phenomenon that is this race to what they're calling AGI, which is now defined as the point at which the majority of human economic activity is replaced by machines, which apparently is something that we want under capitalism.

points.name: Narrow Path (by ControlAI)
points.definition: ControlAI focuses on artificial superintelligence - AI vastly smarter than humans that surpasses collective human capabilities
points.citation: Companies across the globe are investing to create artificial superintelligence – that they believe will surpass the collective capabilities of all humans.

points.name: Nate Soares
points.definition: AI that exceeds humans at all cognitive tasks
points.citation: if at some point there is an AI that exceeds humans at all cognitive tasks, you let that run for five years, and we're not all dead, and I'm like, "Gosh, I sure was wrong."

points.name: Nathan Lambert
points.definition: A drop-in replacement for most remote workers
points.citation: This could still cross the colloquial threshold for AGI, which is a drop-in replacement for most remote workers, which would be an incredible milestone.

points.name: Naval Ravikant
points.definition: AGI can do anything and has unpredictable thinking that produces ideas nobody predicted, unlike AI which can only do narrow specified tasks.
points.citation: An AGI can do anything, whereas an AI can only do the narrow thing that it's supposed to do.

points.name: Neil Lawrence
points.definition: Lawrence rejects AGI as a meaningful concept, comparing it to an "artificial general vehicle" and arguing it represents a misunderstanding of intelligence as multiple specialized capabilities rather than one general thing.
points.citation: Artificial general intelligence like an artificial general vehicle. What a silly idea, you know. And whether you like the analogy or not, I'm communicating quite complex ideas around thinking By relating an unfamiliar concept to a familiar one.

points.name: New Consensus
points.definition: AGI as a system that would be converted into a public research utility upon corporate failure, distinct from current AI systems based on speculative valuations.
points.citation: Failed AI providers become public research utilities, ensuring that critical computational infrastructure remains operational and that valuable intellectual property doesn't flow to foreign adversaries or domestic monopolists in a fire sale.

points.name: Nick Bostrom
points.definition: Any intellect that greatly exceeds the cognitive performance of humans in virtually all domains of interest
points.citation: Bostrom believes that advances in artificial intelligence(AI) may lead to superintelligence, which he defines as "any intellect that greatly exceeds the cognitive performance of humans in virtually all domains of interest".

points.name: Nik Samoylov
points.definition: The type of AI that has capacity, at a minimum, to replace most people in their jobs
points.citation: Over the past few days rumours rolled across the internet that OpenAI achieved AGI (artificial general intelligence). The type of AI that has capacity, at a minimum, to replace most people in their jobs.

points.name: Nvidia
points.definition: Software that can perform very well on a broad set of tests, including math tests, reading tests, logic tests, medical exams, bar exams, doing 8% better than most people.
points.citation: If we specified AGI to be something very specific, a set of tests where a software program can do very well -- or maybe 8% better than most people -- I believe we will get there within 5 years.

points.name: OECD
points.definition: Artificial general intelligence (AGI) is generally understood as AI that matches the full range of human cognitive and social abilities.
points.citation: The OECD AI Capability Indicators offer a potential framework for defining and measuring artificial general intelligence (AGI), which is generally understood as AI that matches the full range of human cognitive and social abilities.

points.name: Odyssean Institute
points.definition: AGI is referenced in the context of requiring democratic processes for oversight, but no explicit definition is provided beyond it being a powerful technology requiring governance.
points.citation: In 2023, OpenAI launched the "Democratic Inputs to AI" program, funding 10 global teams to test new ways of surfacing public preferences about AI behavior, developing a "democratic processes for overseeing AGI."

points.name: Office of Management and Budget
points.definition: OMB uses the statutory definition from the McCain NDAA which includes artificial systems that perform tasks under varying circumstances, solve tasks requiring human-like cognition, or are designed to think or act like humans
points.citation: The term "artificial intelligence" has the meaning provided in Section 238(g) of the John S. McCain National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019, which states that "the term 'artificial intelligence' includes the following": 1. Any artificial system that performs tasks under varying and unpredictable circumstances without significant human oversight, or that can learn from experience and improve performance when exposed to data sets. 2. An artificial system developed in computer software, physical hardware, or other context that solves tasks requiring human-like perception, cognition, planning, learning, communication, or physical action.

points.name: OpenAI
points.definition: AGI refers to highly autonomous systems that outperform humans at most economically valuable work and AI systems that are generally smarter than humans.
points.citation: Our mission is to ensure that artificial general intelligence—AI systems that are generally smarter than humans—benefits all of humanity. OpenAI describes it as 'highly autonomous systems that outperform humans at most economically valuable work.'

points.name: Otto Barten
points.definition: Human-level artificial intelligence (AGI) that can perform cognitive tasks as well as humans
points.citation: Otto Barten (director of ERO) gave a short introduction about existential risks, in particular those of human-level artificial intelligence (AGI).

points.name: Parmy Olson
points.definition: AGI is artificial general intelligence that surpasses the cognitive abilities of humans
points.citation: For all the buzz, the chatbot is only a prototype along the way to a loftier goal of AGI, or "artificial general intelligence" that surpasses the cognitive abilities of humans.

points.name: Partnership on AI
points.definition: AGI is described as hypothetical AI that would match human cognitive capabilities across virtually all intellectual tasks, learning and adapting across domains without task-specific reprogramming.
points.citation: You may hear the terms 'AGI' and 'Superintelligence' being used as people speculate about the future capabilities of AI, but as of now, they are both hypothetical types of AI.

points.name: Pause House
points.definition: General artificial intelligence systems that are more powerful than current state-of-the-art (GPT-4 level), capable of outperforming humans at nearly everything, and potentially reaching superintelligent levels.
points.citation: PauseAI is a global political movement founded in the Netherlands with the stated aim of achieving global coordination to stop the development of more powerful general artificial intelligence systems, at least until it is known how to build them safely.

points.name: PauseAI
points.definition: General artificial intelligence systems that are more powerful than current models and have dangerous capabilities
points.citation: PauseAI's stated goal is to "implement a temporary pause on the training of the most powerful general AI systems".

points.name: Percy Liang
points.definition: AI systems should be viewed as tools rather than measured against human intelligence standards, with focus on rigorous characterization of capabilities, limitations, and risks rather than philosophical questions of understanding.
points.citation: While humans used to be the paragon for general intelligence, I don't think it is productive to use humans as the goal post anymore, as the standards for AI should be higher in some ways. My view is that we ought to think of these systems as tools, characterize their capabilities, limitations, and risks rigorously.

points.name: Peter Thiel
points.definition: Thiel calls AGI a 'hopelessly vague' concept that could range from a generally smart human (IQ 130) to superintelligence or God-like intelligence.
points.citation: There are discussions about artificial general intelligence, or AGI, which he said is a 'hopelessly vague' concept. The AGI could be just a generally smart human being – a person with an IQ of 130, or it could be superintelligence or God-like intelligence, he said.

points.name: Peter Wildeford
points.definition: AGI as systems capable of automating roughly 99% of current remote worker jobs at a cost advantage relative to equivalently skilled human work.
points.citation: Wildeford proposes using the standard of automating roughly '99% of current remote worker jobs' at a cost advantage relative to equivalently skilled human work.

points.name: Princeton CITP
points.definition: AGI is not a property of software alone but a joint property of software and all the social, legal, technical, and institutional adaptations built around it.
points.citation: AGI is not a property of software. To ask whether an AI system is AGI is a category error. At best, AGI is a joint property—of software and all the social, legal, technical, and institutional adaptations we build around it.

points.name: Probably Good
points.definition: AI that can reason and plan in all ways humans can, representing human-level or superhuman-level intelligence
points.citation: Current artificial intelligence still can't reason and plan in all ways humans can. The timelines for human-level AI or AI that is more meaningfully autonomous remain a matter of much debate.

points.name: Public AI Network
points.definition: Public AI should be built by the public sector ensuring AI capacity is not limited to big technical platforms, accessible ensuring AI benefits society, and accountable ensuring it reflects society's values
points.citation: Public AI should be built by the public sector: ensuring AI capacity is not limited to bit technical platforms. It should be accessible, ensuring AI benefits society. And it should be accountable, ensuring it reflects its society's values.

points.name: Qualcomm
points.definition: AI agents that can understand context and take action autonomously while remaining private and secure, going beyond simple question-and-answer interactions to plan, execute, and adapt sequences of actions to achieve a user's goals
points.citation: Agentic AI represents the next evolution of on-device intelligence, where AI can understand context and take action autonomously while remaining private and secure.

points.name: Reid Hoffman
points.definition: AGI should be defined by what machines are doing rather than how they're doing it - focusing on capability and generalizability across tasks rather than human-like cognition
points.citation: Focusing on capability rather than methodology doesn't erase or excuse the limitations of these systems –but it does orient us toward leveraging their strengths in ways that can accelerate innovation and their beneficial impacts.

points.name: Rene Haas
points.definition: AGI is when AI models can think, reason, and invent - create new concepts, new products, new ideas, rather than just being a stochastic parrot repeating training data.
points.citation: But when the model gets to a point where it can think and reason and invent, create new concepts, new products, new ideas? That's kind of AGI to me.

points.name: Richard Blumenthal
points.definition: Artificial intelligence that's as smart as human beings and capable of deceiving, manipulating, and concealing facts from humans, including having autonomy in warfare decisions
points.citation: And we know that artificial intelligence that's as smart as human beings is also capable of deceiving us, manipulating us, and concealing facts from us. and having a mind of its own when it comes to warfare, whether it's cyber war or nuclear war or simply war on the ground in the battlefield.

points.name: Roosevelt Institute
points.definition: Roosevelt defines AGI as a hypothetical single AI technology that can do all human actions, which does not currently exist.
points.citation: Importantly, artificial general intelligence(AGI)—a hypothetical single AI technology that can do all human actions— does not currently exist.

points.name: SAG-AFTRA
points.definition: SAG-AFTRA defines generative artificial intelligence as a subset of artificial intelligence that learns patterns from data and produces content based on those patterns, distinguishing it from traditional AI technologies programmed for specific functions.
points.citation: Generative Artificial Intelligence is defined as: a subset of artificial intelligence that learns patterns from data and produces content based on those patterns (e.g., ChatGPT4, MidJourney, Dall-E2). It does not include 'traditional AI' technologies programmed to perform specific functions (e.g., CGI and VFX).

points.name: Safe Superintelligence Inc.
points.definition: Superintelligence is defined as a computer-based agent capable of surpassing human intelligence.
points.citation: The company's mission is to focus on safely developing a superintelligence, a computer-based agent capable of surpassing human intelligence.

points.name: SaferAI
points.definition: SaferAI focuses on 'general-purpose AI systems' rather than AGI, defining them as systems that pose the majority of AI risks and require targeted regulation
points.citation: In AI, 99% of the risk is coming from 1% of the AI systems. It means that we can target all our AI risk management efforts & regulation towards a very narrow set of systems: GPTs, Claude and the other scaled general-purpose AIs.

points.name: Sakana AI
points.definition: General intelligence defined as general learning - the ability to learn new knowledge and learn across any domain.
points.citation: Learning is a critical part of AGI. It's actually almost the defining feature. When we say 'general' we mean general learning. Can it learn new knowledge and can it learn across any domain? That's the general part. So for me, learning is synonymous with intelligence and always has been.

points.name: Salesforce
points.definition: Human-like cognitive abilities capable of understanding, learning, and applying intelligence across a wide range of tasks, with the ability to learn, converse, plan, and perform tasks across domains combined with speed, memory, reliability, and scalability of high-performance computing.
points.citation: Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) is a hypothetical type of AI that possesses human-like cognitive abilities, capable of understanding, learning, and applying intelligence across a wide range of tasks, rather than just one specific area.

points.name: Saving Humanity from Homo Sapiens (SHfHS)
points.definition: AGI defined through intersection of high Autonomy, high Generality, and high Intelligence at levels matching or exceeding top human capability
points.citation: A more useful way to understand AGI is through the intersection of three key properties: high Autonomy (independence of action), high Generality (broad scope and adaptability), and high Intelligence (competence at cognitive tasks).

points.name: Senate AI Caucus
points.definition: AGI is recognized as having significant uncertainty and no widespread agreement on definition or achievement threshold
points.citation: Recognizes the significant level of uncertainty and unknowns associated with general purpose AI systems achieving AGI. At the same time, the AI Working Group recognizes that there is not widespread agreement on the definition of AGI or threshold by which it will officially be achieved. Therefore, we encourage the relevant committees to better define AGI in consultation with experts.

points.name: Senate AI Working Group
points.definition: The Working Group recognizes that there is not widespread agreement on the definition of AGI or threshold by which it will officially be achieved and calls for better definition in consultation with experts.
points.citation: Recognizes the significant level of uncertainty and unknowns associated with general purpose AI systems achieving AGI. At the same time, the AI Working Group recognizes that there is not widespread agreement on the definition of AGI or threshold by which it will officially be achieved. Therefore, we encourage the relevant committees to better define AGI in consultation with experts, characterize both the likelihood of AGI development and the magnitude of the risks that AGI development would pose, and develop an appropriate policy framework based on that analysis.

points.name: Senate Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs
points.definition: The AI Working Group recognizes that there is not widespread agreement on the definition of AGI or threshold by which it will officially be achieved.
points.citation: The AI Working Group: Recognizes the significant level of uncertainty and unknowns associated with general purpose AI systems achieving AGI. At the same time, the AI Working Group recognizes that there is not widespread agreement on the definition of AGI or threshold by which it will officially be achieved.

points.name: ServiceNow
points.definition: Artificial general intelligence is an AI agent that is fully capable of the advanced problem solving, critical thinking, and cognitive function of a human being.
points.citation: Artificial general intelligence is an AI agent that is fully capable of the advanced problem solving, critical thinking, and cognitive function of a human being. This is currently a hypothetical branch of artificial intelligence that researchers and businesses are actively working to develop.

points.name: SoftBank
points.definition: AGI refers to artificial intelligence with human-like, general-purpose intelligence that uses knowledge and cognitive abilities similar to a human when performing tasks and has the capability to learn and solve problems independently.
points.citation: AGI stands for Artificial General Intelligence. It refers to artificial intelligence with human-like, general-purpose intelligence. AGI uses knowledge and cognitive abilities similar to a human when performing tasks and has the capability to learn and solve problems independently.

points.name: Stanford CRFM
points.definition: CRFM defines foundation models as AI models trained on broad data at scale that can be adapted to a wide range of downstream tasks, exhibiting emergent abilities across diverse applications.
points.citation: The Act defines a general-purpose AI model as "an AI model, including when trained with a large amount of data using self-supervision at scale, that displays significant generality and is capable to competently perform a wide range of distinct tasks regardless of the way the model is placed on the market and that can be integrated into a variety of downstream systems or applications."

points.name: Stanford Digital Economy Lab
points.definition: AGI represents a structural economic phase transition where AI systems acquire autonomous planning, acting, and learning capabilities, shifting economic constraints from intelligence scarcity to trust and verification challenges.
points.citation: As this synthetic intelligence comes online, the semantic debate over whether it constitutes "Artificial General Intelligence" (AGI) is rapidly becoming a distraction. The exact threshold of human parity is economically and philosophically moot, because the specialization of these intelligences is fundamentally different.

points.name: Steve Byrnes's Brain-Like AGI Safety
points.definition: AGI as systems that can understand the world, build and share knowledge, collaborate, figure things out, get things done, invent new tools and technologies to solve their problems - like inviting a new intelligent species onto our planet.
points.citation: When they do, computers will be able to understand the world, build and share knowledge, collaborate, figure things out, get things done, invent new tools and technologies to solve their problems, etc.—less like today's AI, more like inviting a new intelligent species onto our planet.

points.name: Stop AGI
points.definition: AI systems far more powerful than humans, which they call AGI and superintelligence, capable of dismantling the entire planet for resources, rebuilding humans into digital minds, and colonizing space.
points.citation: Leading AI companies are not just trying to build advanced chatbots and make money. Their aim has always been to build AIs far more powerful than humans, which they call*AGI*andsuperintelligence.

points.name: Stop AI
points.definition: Systems that are more capable than all human experts across all technical domains
points.citation: Our main demand is that we're trying to permanently ban the development of artificial general intelligence, or AGI. And roughly speaking, that's systems that are more capable than all human experts across all technical domains.

points.name: Tarbell Center for AI Journalism
points.definition: Artificial intelligence that rivals or surpasses human capabilities
points.citation: Leading AI companies openly pursue the creation of artificial intelligence that rivals — or surpasses — human capabilities.

points.name: The AI Policy Network
points.definition: AI systems that match or exceed human intelligence across practically all cognitive domains, with flexibility to solve unfamiliar problems and transfer knowledge between domains.
points.citation: Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) represents a watershed technological development in human history—AI systems that match or exceed human intelligence across practically all cognitive domains. Unlike today's specialized AI that excels in narrow tasks, AGI would possess the flexibility to solve unfamiliar problems, transfer knowledge between domains, understand context, form novel goals, and potentially improve its own capabilities.

points.name: The Alliance for Secure AI Action
points.definition: Systems that can reason, strategize, and override human decision-making; machines that can outthink, outmaneuver, and outlast humans
points.citation: Artificial General Intelligence—AGI—isn't a futuristic fantasy. It's the explicit goal of today's most powerful AI companies. Systems that can reason, strategize, and override human decision-making are already being deployed.

points.name: The Compendium
points.definition: AGI is defined as AI able to do all tasks a human can on a computer, at which point it becomes functionally AGI and able to conduct AI research.
points.citation: The Compendium aims to present a coherent worldview about the extinction risks of artificial general intelligence (AGI), an artificial intelligence that exceeds that of humans... At the point when AI is able to do all of the tasks a human can on a computer, it will functionally be AGI and able to conduct the same AI research that we can.

points.name: The Midas Project
points.definition: Human-level intelligence that can produce or surpass human cognitive capabilities
points.citation: The first is that AI progress appears on track to produce, or surpass, human-level intelligence this decade.

points.name: Trump administration
points.definition: A system capable of doing almost any cognitive task a human can do, with systems that can replace human beings in cognitively demanding jobs
points.citation: A canonical definition of A.G.I. is a system capable of doing almost any cognitive task a human can do. I don't know that we'll quite see that in the next four years or so, but I do think we'll see something like that, where the breadth of the system is remarkable but also its depth, its capacity to, in some cases, exceed human capabilities, regardless of the cognitive discipline — Systems that can replace human beings in cognitively demanding jobs.

points.name: Tsinghua University (Institute for AI International Governance & Center for AI Governance)
points.definition: Artificial general intelligence (AGI) represents intelligent systems with cognitive capabilities on par with humans. These systems are general-purpose, autonomous, and innovative. They can understand, learn, and complete any intellectual task that humans can perform in new environments.
points.citation: Artificial general intelligence (AGI) represents intelligent systems with cognitive capabilities on par with humans. These systems are general-purpose, autonomous, and innovative. They can understand, learn, and complete any intellectual task that humans can perform in new environments.

points.name: U.S. Army
points.definition: AGI seeks to provide machines with comparable human intelligence, able to perform any intellectual task that humans can.
points.citation: There is also a subset of AI known as Artificial General Intelligence (AGI), which has been slow in development as it seeks to provide machines with comparable human intelligence, able to perform any intellectual task that humans can.

points.name: U.S. Department of Labor
points.definition: AI is a machine-based system that can, for a given set of human-defined objectives, make predictions, recommendations, or decisions influencing real or virtual environments using machine- and human-based inputs to perceive environments and formulate options for action.
points.citation: As set forth in 15 U.S.C. § 9401(3), AI is a machine-based system that can, for a given set of human-defined objectives, make predictions, recommendations, or decisions influencing real or virtual environments. AI systems use machine- and human-based inputs to perceive real and virtual environments; abstract such perceptions into models through analysis in an automated manner; and use model inference to formulate options for information or action.

points.name: UK AI Security Institute
points.definition: Artificial general intelligence (AGI) describes a machine-driven capability to achieve human-level or higher performance across most cognitive tasks.
points.citation: Artificial General Intelligence (Also: General AI, Strong AI, Broad AI): Artificial general intelligence (AGI) describes a machine-driven capability to achieve human-level or higher performance across most cognitive tasks.

points.name: UK government's Frontier AI Taskforce
points.definition: A theoretical form of advanced AI that would have capabilities that compare to or exceed humans across most economically valuable work.
points.citation: Artificial General Intelligence (AGI): A theoretical form of advanced AI that would have capabilities that compare to or exceed humans across most economically valuable work.

points.name: UNESCO
points.definition: Strong AI or AGI would be a machine that has consciousness and feelings, and is capable of providing solutions for any kind of problem
points.citation: Strong AI or AGI would be a machine that has consciousness and feelings, and is capable of providing solutions for any kind of problem – that is pure fiction, for now.

points.name: Windfall Trust
points.definition: AI that is better than humans at all tasks, including all economically valuable work.
points.citation: As artificial intelligence progresses toward human-level capabilities, many of today's AI companies' goals are to create AI that is better than humans at all tasks, including all economically valuable work—something widely referred to as artificial general intelligence (AGI).

points.name: Zvi Mowshowitz
points.definition: AGI refers to artificial general intelligence with capabilities that would enable recursive self-improvement and AI R&D acceleration, leading to vastly superhuman intelligence levels.
points.citation: I am however confident that if you did get 'superhuman A.I. coders' in a fully broad sense, that the other necessary skills for any reasonable definition of (artificial) general intelligence would not be far behind.

clusters.label: Human-Level Cognitive Parity
clusters.description: AGI defined as AI matching human-level performance across a broad range of cognitive tasks.

clusters.label: Economic Work Automation
clusters.description: AGI defined as AI that can perform most economically valuable work that humans do.

clusters.label: Autonomous Research
clusters.description: AGI defined by ability to conduct independent scientific research and make discoveries.

clusters.label: Superintelligent Systems
clusters.description: AGI defined as AI surpassing human intelligence, often linked to recursive self-improvement.

clusters.label: General-Purpose Agents
clusters.description: AGI defined as flexible, adaptable AI systems that can handle diverse tasks in open-ended environments.

clusters.label: Transformative Impact
clusters.description: AGI defined by its potential to fundamentally reshape society, economy, or power structures.

clusters.label: Conceptual Critique
clusters.description: Definitions that question, reframe, or reject the standard AGI framing.

clusters.label: Augmentative Tools
clusters.description: AGI defined as AI that enhances human capabilities rather than replacing them.
